timeline.label: Q1 FY2026 revenue $10.3B (+36% YoY quarter); Data Center momentum continues per 10-Q
FY2025 revenue $34.6B (+34%); Data Center $16.6B (+32%); FCF ~$5.5B on continuing ops
Instinct MI300 GPU ramp drives Data Center to 48% of revenue; AI accelerator share gains vs NVIDIA
FY2024 revenue $25.8B; Data Center recovery accelerates post-2023 trough
Xilinx acquisition closes (~$49B); adds Embedded adaptive SoC and FPGA franchise
Post-Xilinx AMD becomes multi-segment silicon platform: Data Center, Client/Gaming, Embedded
Announced Xilinx all-stock acquisition; largest strategic bet on adaptive computing
Lisa Su CEO era; pivot from PC-centric to data center and high-performance computing focus
Zen x86 architecture launch restores competitive server and client CPU position vs Intel
GlobalFoundries spin-off; AMD becomes fabless, TSMC-dependent for advanced nodes

industry.structure: Advanced semiconductors is a concentrated oligopoly: NVIDIA dominates AI GPUs; Intel and AMD compete in x86 CPUs; TSMC fabricates most leading-edge chips. High R&D, long design cycles, and ecosystem lock-in (CUDA vs ROCm) create entry barriers.
industry.share_shift: AMD is gaining share in server CPUs (EPYC) and AI accelerators (Instinct MI300) as the credible second source; NVIDIA retains CUDA software moat; hyperscalers invest in custom ASICs.
industry.trend: AI data-center build-out drives demand for GPUs, HBM memory, and advanced packaging. Export controls limit China revenue. Foundry capacity allocation and capex step-ups are the main supply constraints.
